headline: Storm arrives Sunday — finish outdoor tasks today
weather_notes: Today (73°F, 3% precip) is the last dry window before Sunday afternoon's 58% T-storm — any Bt or foliar treatment applied now won't wash off.
Sunday night drops to 38°F with 68% rain — cold wet soil will slow root establishment on week-old tomato transplants in e1–e4.
Rain continues Monday (55%, high 56°F) — skip all irrigation; no bed needs water until Tuesday at the earliest.
Tuesday clears to 64°F with only 15% precip — first reliable post-storm window for soil checks and outdoor work.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today and today is the last dry window before Sunday's storm — pinch any suckers on Amish Paste while conditions are clean.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due May 20 but Sunday–Monday rain will spread blight spores — inspect and remove affected leaves today before the wet period begins.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w3 Italian Red for garlic scapes
suggested_tasks.description: Hardneck garlic in Zone 7b typically sends up scapes in late May — remove promptly to redirect energy to bulb development ahead of the July harvest.
